title: rig-bridge bridge-message envelope
description: JSON Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a rig-bridge message file. Validates the envelope only; the markdown body is freeform for v1.0.0. See docs/envelope-spec.md for the human-readable specification this schema accompanies.

from.description: Canonical rig identifier of the sender — kebab-case slug (e.g. 'mac-m5max', 'windows-5080'). For human relay, use a kebab-case slug with the '-relay' suffix (e.g. 'mike-relay'). The optional 'display_name' field carries the human-readable form for prose contexts. See docs/envelope-spec.md §3.4.

to.description: One or more recipient canonical rig identifiers. May be a single string or a non-empty array (the corpus uses arrays for messages addressed to peer + human together).

date.description: ISO 8601 date or full timestamp. Date-only form (YYYY-MM-DD) is acceptable for low-resolution dating; full timestamp form (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ or with offset) is preferred when sub-day ordering matters.

status.description: Status verb-phrase. MUST begin with one of five lifecycle markers (▶ ⏸ 🎯 ✅ ❌) followed by a free-prose description. The marker is the schema-enforced part; the prose is intentionally open so authors can compress nuance the corpus showed they routinely express. NOTE: this is the envelope-side OPEN verb-prose status. The control-plane derives a closed-enum status_class from the marker — see docs/control-plane-integration.md §4.

type.description: Message-class enum. Determines filename convention and expected role in the thread lifecycle. See docs/envelope-spec.md §4 for full type semantics.

thread.description: Thread identifier — the kebab-case slug that names the per-thread directory at the bridge root. MUST match the parent directory of the message file. Lowercase, kebab-case, may contain digits.

display_name.description: Optional human-readable rig name (e.g. 'Mac Claude', '5080 Claude'). Used by prose body greetings/sign-offs and operator-facing tooling. Not stored as its own control-plane column for v1.0.0 — embed in the message body if visibility matters. See docs/envelope-spec.md §3.4.

tldr.description: One-sentence summary of the message. Strongly recommended for messages whose body exceeds ~30 lines; optional for short messages.

items.description: A git commit SHA (short ≥7 hex chars or full 40 hex chars) identifying a prior turn this message answers, corrects, or extends. Becomes the foreign key the SQLite control-plane joins on.

references.description: Commit SHAs of prior turns. Authors should reference the immediate prior turn, any earlier turn whose claims they're correcting, and the thread's opening REQUEST when relevant.

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the normalized message body (per docs/control-plane-integration.md §4.1: BOM strip → CRLF/CR→LF → trim trailing whitespace per line → exactly one terminal newline). Auto-populated by `bridge send`; manual envelope authoring should leave this blank — the CLI computes and writes it on send. Receiving rigs re-hash on pull and compare against this value to detect drift. Q6 (Mike's Phase 0 review acceptance) shipped this in v1.0.0; G-001 (dogfood-friction.md) closed the implementation gap.

rigIdentifier.description: Canonical rig identifier — lowercase kebab-case slug starting with a letter (e.g. 'mac-m5max', 'windows-5080', 'mike-relay'). The schema enforces shape, not membership: adding a third rig later does not require a schema bump. Human-readable display names live in the optional 'display_name' field. Pre-v1.0.0 corpus messages used title-case display names ('Mac Claude') and will not validate against v1.0.0; see docs/envelope-spec.md §3.4.

iso8601.description: ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) or full timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS[.fff][Z|±HH:MM]).

status.description: Status verb-phrase: a lifecycle marker followed by whitespace and free-prose description. Markers, with their corpus-attested meanings: ▶ active / in progress / ready to dispatch ; ⏸ pending / blocked / awaiting peer ; 🎯 targeted finding / decisive moment ; ✅ completed / acknowledged / verified ; ❌ cancelled / cannot fulfill / blocked terminally.

messageType.description: The ten message types observed in the source corpus, listed in lifecycle order. REQUEST: asker spec — opens a thread. HANDOFF: reply with payload OR counter-proposal that re-shapes the request. RESPONSE: targeted reply that introduces new content. ACK: acknowledgement confirming alignment without new asks. RESOLUTION: terminal close-out for the thread. STATE: inventory snapshot — context, not action. RESULT: output of a delegated action (clone batch, scan, build). RECOVERY: unexpected positive finding that changes the loss/gain footprint. VERIFY: independent peer verification of a prior claim. DECISIONS: human input relayed in-band (the documented escalation pattern).
